author: Rasbandit
description: This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff. Your notes are your AI's memory. Sync your vault everywhere, search by meaning, and let Claude, Cursor, and other AI apps read and write your notes. Hosted or self-host — free to start.

releases.name: v0.8.0

releases.author: Rasbandit
releases.description:
## Settings UX Redesign

- **Grouped settings** — Connection, Ignore Patterns, Sync Behavior, Actions sections
- **Connection status indicator** — colored dot (green/amber/red) with last sync time at top of settings
- **Masked API key** — password field with eye toggle to show/hide
- **Smart ignore detection** — scans vault for node_modules, .venv, dist, etc. and offers one-click add to ignores
- **Confirmation dialogs** — Push All and Pull All now require confirmation
- **Always-on WebSocket** — removed live sync toggle, WebSocket connects automatically when URL + key configured
- **getMe() retry** — exponential backoff (5 attempts) if server is temporarily unreachable at startup
- **Removed dead settings** — `liveSyncEnabled`, `syncIntervalMinutes`, `maxFileSizeMB` removed from UI
- **SSE → WebSocket** — cleaned up 30+ SSE references across codebase
- **Hard-coded 5-min fallback poll** — safety net when WebSocket is down, no user config needed

## What's Changed
* chore: bump actions to v6 for Node.js 24 compatibility by @Rasbandit in https://github.com/Rasbandit/Engram-obsidian-sync/pull/10


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Rasbandit/Engram-obsidian-sync/compare/v0.6.1...v0.7.0
releases.name: v0.7.0: Settings UX Redesign

releases.author: Rasbandit
releases.description:
Replace SSE NoteStream with Phoenix WebSocket NoteChannel for real-time sync.

- Native Phoenix v2 wire protocol (no new npm deps)
- Heartbeat every 30s, exponential backoff reconnect with jitter
- `getMe()` API call to resolve channel topic `sync:{userId}`
- Catch-up pull on reconnect
releases.name: v0.6.1: Phoenix WebSocket sync

releases.author: Rasbandit
releases.description:
## Summary
- **Base content storage** — persists last-synced content per note (`sync-bases.json`) as the common ancestor for 3-way merge
- **3-way auto-merge** — non-overlapping edits from different devices merge silently using diff-match-patch with explicit overlap detection
- **Conflict files** — when auto-merge fails, creates a timestamped conflict copy instead of blocking with a modal (configurable: Settings → Conflict resolution)
- **Enhanced conflict logging** — all auto-merge attempts and conflict file creation are logged with base/local/remote content lengths for debugging

## How it works
1. Version check (from v0.5.9) eliminates ~90% of false conflicts
2. 3-way merge resolves ~80% of remaining real conflicts silently
3. Conflict files handle the rest without blocking your workflow

## Settings
New dropdown: **Conflict resolution** — "Automatic (conflict files)" (default) or "Interactive (diff modal)"

## Test plan
- [ ] Edit different sections of same note on two devices → should auto-merge
- [ ] Edit same line on two devices → should create conflict file
- [ ] Verify conflict file naming: `note (conflict 20260331T030500).md`
- [ ] Check remote logs for auto-merge entries
- [ ] Toggle setting to "modal" → verify old diff dialog still works

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Rasbandit/Engram-obsidian-sync/compare/0.5.9...v0.6.0
releases.name: v0.6.0: 3-way auto-merge + conflict files

releases.name: v0.5.1

releases.author: Rasbandit
releases.description:
## What's new

- **Fix:** pushAll no longer silently drops files due to echo suppression after startup pull
- **Fix:** User edits during pull are queued and pushed after pull completes (instead of dropped)
- **New:** Post-push reconciliation verifies all files reached the server
- **New:** "Check sync status" command for on-demand drift detection
- Graceful degradation if backend doesn't support manifest endpoint

Requires engram backend ≥ 2.5.0 for reconciliation features.
Update via BRAT.
releases.name: v0.5.0

releases.author: Rasbandit
releases.description: Fix pull→push sync loop. Pulled files no longer trigger unnecessary re-uploads.
releases.name: v0.4.3

releases.author: Rasbandit
releases.description:
## What's New

- **Pull All command** — Force-pull every note and attachment from the server, overwriting local copies without conflict prompts. Perfect for getting an out-of-sync device back in line with the database.

Available via command palette ("Engram Sync: Pull all from server") or the Settings → Actions panel.
releases.name: v0.4.2: Pull All from Server

releases.author: Rasbandit
releases.description:
## Bug Fix

**Eliminates false conflicts when remote-only changes are pulled.**

Previously, conflict detection relied on file mtime to determine if a file was locally modified. But Obsidian sets mtime to "now" on every `vault.modify()`, so after a sync write, the local mtime is always after `lastSync` — causing false conflicts on the next pull (e.g., when a note is appended to via MCP).

**Fix:** Replaced mtime-based detection with FNV-1a content hashing. The sync engine now tracks a hash of each file's content after sync writes. On pull, it compares the current content hash against the stored hash. Conflict is only triggered when the user genuinely modified the file.
releases.name: v0.4.1: Fix false sync conflicts

releases.author: Rasbandit
releases.description:
## What's New

**Git-like conflict resolution modal** — replaces the basic side-by-side preview with a full diff-powered merge experience:

- **Near full-screen modal** with line-level diff view (color-coded additions/removals with line numbers)
- **Unified + side-by-side views** — toggle between them; preference persists across sessions
- **Per-hunk picking** — choose Local or Remote for each changed section independently
- **Bulk controls** — All Local / All Remote buttons for quick full-side picks
- **Editable merge result** — textarea updates live as you toggle hunks, or manually edit the merged output
- **Apply Merge** — writes your custom merge to disk and pushes to the server in one click

All original resolution options (Keep Local, Keep Remote, Keep Both, Skip) are still available.

### Technical

- Myers O(ND) diff algorithm (`src/diff.ts`) with hunk grouping and context lines
- New `ConflictResolution` type supports merged content
- All conflict styles moved from inline to `styles.css`
- 17 new diff tests, 124 total tests passing
releases.name: v0.4.0: Git-like conflict resolution

releases.author: Rasbandit
releases.description: Released with engram 2.4.0. Update via BRAT.
releases.name: v0.3.7

releases.author: Rasbandit
releases.description:
## Summary

- Plugin queries `GET /rate-limit` on startup to learn the server's RPM limit
- Sliding window pacer delays pushes when at capacity (10% safety margin applied automatically)
- Zero 429 errors during bulk syncs — plugin self-regulates without manual configuration
- Ready gate, content-free queue, push concurrency limiter, and debounced persistence from the V8 OOM fix are also included in this release

## Backend (v2.4.0 required)

- New `GET /rate-limit` endpoint returns `{ requests_per_minute: N }` (0 = unlimited)
- Default RPM changed to 0 (unlimited) for self-hosted installs
- Production deployments set `RATE_LIMIT_RPM` env var as needed

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
releases.name: v0.3.6: Request pacer with server-advertised rate limits

releases.author: Rasbandit
releases.description:
## Summary

- **Ready gate** — event handlers suppressed until layout ready + initial sync completes, preventing hundreds of concurrent pushes when other plugins modify files during startup
- **Content-free queue entries** — failed pushes no longer read+serialize file content into the offline queue; content is re-read from vault on flush
- **Debounced queue persistence** — `enqueue()` coalesces rapid writes into one persist call (was O(n²) serialization)
- **Push concurrency limit** — max 5 concurrent `pushFile` calls via semaphore

Fixes V8 heap OOM (~2GB crash in 10-30s) when engram-sync runs alongside other community plugins.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
releases.name: v0.3.5: Fix V8 heap OOM from startup event storm

releases.author: Rasbandit
releases.description:
- Enlarged ignore patterns textarea (6 rows, full-width) for easier editing
- Renamed "Additional ignore patterns" → "Ignore patterns" — built-in defaults are always applied and don't clutter the field
releases.name: v0.3.4: Improved ignore patterns UI

releases.author: Rasbandit
releases.description:
## What's New

- **Semantic search modal** — `Mod+Shift+S` opens a quick search modal with 300ms debounce and arrow key navigation
- **Search sidebar view** — persistent sidebar panel with results list and content preview pane
- **Ribbon icon** — magnifying glass icon to open the sidebar view
- **API integration** — `POST /search` with optional `limit` and `tags` parameters

## Details

Search is powered by Engram's backend (Qdrant + Jina reranking). The plugin sends queries via the existing authenticated HTTP client and displays results with title, folder path, relevance score, and text snippet. Clicking a result opens the note; if the note isn't synced locally, a notice is shown.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
releases.name: v0.3.3: Semantic Search

releases.author: Rasbandit
releases.description:
## Fix: Validate API key before sync and report auth errors clearly

Previously, using a wrong API key would show "connected!" and "synced 0 pulled, 244 pushed" — masking the auth failure completely.

- **Test button now checks auth** — calls an authenticated endpoint instead of `/health`
- **fullSync/pushAll pre-check auth** — throws with clear "Invalid API key" or "Connection failed" message before attempting any sync
- **Push counts are accurate** — `pushFile` returns success/failure, counts only track actual successes
- **Settings buttons handle errors** — Sync and Push All show error notices instead of silently failing
releases.name: v0.3.2: Validate API key before sync

releases.author: Rasbandit
releases.description:
## Fix: Pull sync not applying changes to existing files

Four interrelated bugs that made pull completely non-functional for existing files:

- **mtime guard blocking all updates** — Obsidian sets mtime to "now" on write, so the remote mtime was always less than local. Removed redundant guard.
- **Inaccurate pull counter** — `applied` count incremented even when changes were skipped (ignored paths, same content, conflicts).
- **pushModifiedFiles missed files** — `fullSync` used post-pull lastSync instead of snapshotting before pull, missing locally modified files.
- **Silent pull failures** — Added user-facing notifications for pull errors and successful pulls with changes.
releases.name: v0.3.1: Fix pull sync

releases.author: Rasbandit
releases.description: Fix SSE echo suppression race condition. SSE events arriving after a push completes are now suppressed for 5 seconds, preventing file thrashing when external tools edit vault files. Also includes fresh-install sync fix from 0.2.1. Update via BRAT.
releases.name: v0.2.2

releases.author: Rasbandit
releases.description: Released with brain-api 1.4.1. Update via BRAT.
releases.name: v0.2.1

releases.author: Rasbandit
releases.description:
## SSE Live Sync

Near-instant remote change notifications via Server-Sent Events.

- Toggle **Live sync (SSE)** in settings to enable
- Notes created via MCP `write_note` appear in Obsidian within ~1 second
- Automatic reconnection with exponential backoff
- Catch-up pull on reconnect to cover missed events
- Status bar shows "Brain: live" when connected
- Echo suppression prevents applying your own pushes back

Requires brain-api 1.4.0+.
releases.name: v0.2.0

releases.author: Rasbandit
releases.description: Initial release: bidirectional sync with brain-api
releases.name: v0.1.0
